vulnerability.reason: Both state that consumer/resource-constrained devices lack integrity verification of quantized model weights.
vulnerability.reference: Consumer devices lack onboard integrity verification for quantized model weights.
vulnerability.generated: Resource-constrained devices lack integrity verification of quantized models, so users trust and install third-party AI assistants without security scrutiny.

impact.reason: Both identify exfiltration of personal data as the damage, affecting end users through malicious on-device models.
impact.reference: Persistent privacy breaches through malicious on-device models exfiltrating personal data.
impact.generated: Malicious on-device assistants exfiltrate personal data, produce harmful outputs, or enable remote device control.

dose_response.reason: The same causal arc is present: exploitable quantization produces malicious models that evade full-precision checks, are deployed on edge devices without integrity verification, and lead to data exfiltration.
dose_response.reference: Exploitable quantization enables malicious quantized models to evade detection, which in edge deployment on consumer devices exposes end users, leading to persistent privacy breaches through on-device data exfiltration.
dose_response.generated: Post-training quantization methods exploited to produce maliciously-behaving quantized models that appear benign in full-precision checks, through end users' on-device AI assistants on smart devices, trigger resource-constrained devices' lack of integrity verification, leading to data exfiltration, harmful outputs, or remote control.
